tips: Write tests before code — Test-Driven Development leads to better APIs
Use describe/it blocks to organize tests into readable specifications
Test edge cases: empty inputs, null values, boundary conditions
Mock external services to make tests fast and deterministic
Use code coverage reports to find untested paths in your codebase
Snapshot testing catches unintended UI or output changes
Run tests in CI to catch regressions before they reach production
Use property-based testing to find edge cases you didn't think of
Keep tests independent — each test should set up and tear down its own state
Name tests as sentences: 'it returns 404 when user is not found'
Use bun test for a fast, built-in test runner with TypeScript support
Test error paths, not just the happy path
